finding: KB lessons: prompt caching with 1hr TTL is highest-impact for large-input agentic workloads; max_tokens=500 reduces quota 8x; parallel investigation overhead for trivial questions is wasteful - assess convergence first

Multi-Agent Debate (MAD) literature confirms: early termination, adaptive rounds, and direct API calls (vs subprocess) are key optimizations. Paper arxiv 2511.11306 (AAAI 2026 Oral) specifically addresses efficient MAD inference.
Bedrock Converse API + ConverseStream enables direct LLM calls with streaming, multi-turn, tool use, and prompt caching - eliminates kiro-cli subprocess overhead entirely
Source code analyzed: 10 contentions spawned from opening, child contentions spawn within first round, each kiro-cli call takes 30-300s, full exchange history grows per round

Prompt caching on Bedrock: 1024-token min for Claude 3.7 Sonnet, 4096 for newer models. 5-min TTL (resets on hit), 1-hour TTL available. Up to 4 cache checkpoints per request. Cache system+messages+tools. PERFECT for the growing ESTABLISHED TRUTHS context.

Key paper: iMAD (AAAI 2026) selectively triggers debate ONLY when beneficial - learns when debate will correct wrong answers vs waste tokens. Also: D3 framework uses cost-aware budgeting with advocates+judge+jury. Prompt caching reduces costs 45-80% and latency 13-31% per arxiv 2601.06007.

CRITICAL STATS: 721 agent calls, median 83s each, max 5614s. 56 children spawned across depths 1-7. 28 judged + 20 agreed = 48 resolved. 13/28 judged as both_correct (46%) = wasted debate cycles. Depth distribution: d1=14, d2=8, d3=11, d4=11, d5=6, d6=5, d7=1

Found Consensus Engine multi-agent debate framework (EE Science Hackathon 2025) - uses persona engine + debate orchestrator + consensus engine with structured protocols. Also found AGI-DS Quality Auditing Assistant using multi-agent debate with affirmative/negative agents + confidence scores. Key blog: Plumbing Not People argues single-agent with skills outperforms multi-agent systems.

AWS Prescriptive Guidance multi-agent pattern: use SQS/EventBridge for agent communication, DynamoDB/S3 for shared memory (blackboard), Step Functions for orchestration with timeout/retry. Supervisor-collaborator hierarchy matches debate judge pattern.

CRITICAL: iMAD paper (arxiv 2511.11306) shows MAD uses 3-5x more tokens than single-agent with only 1.5-5.3% accuracy gain. Selective triggering via self-critique + classifier reduces tokens 92% while improving accuracy 13.5%. Key: dont debate everything, use convergence detection.

Critical finding: arxiv 2605.00914 shows isolated self-correction OUTPERFORMS unguided homogeneous multi-agent debate. Models frequently shift from correct to incorrect answers in response to peer reasoning (arxiv 2509.05396). Debate should be selective, not universal.

D3/SAMRE paper: 4-round debate with early stopping (if judge agrees 2 consecutive rounds) + jury achieves 5-8% accuracy gain over baseline. Multi-advocate parallel (MORE) is faster than iterative single-advocate. Key: stop when judge scores agree in same direction for 2 rounds.

CRITICAL: Problem Drift paper (EACL 2026) analyzed 170 debates - found lack of progress (35%), low-quality feedback (26%), lack of clarity (25%) as main causes. Agent drift paper shows progressive degradation over extended interactions. 7 rounds is likely TOO MANY - causes quality degradation.

Adaptive Stability Detection (NeurIPS 2025): Use KS-statistic on Beta-Binomial mixture to detect when debate has converged. Threshold 0.05 for 2 consecutive rounds. Reduces 10 rounds to 4-6 with <1% accuracy loss. Ensemble size 7 is optimal.

CRITICAL: Claude 3.7+ has 5x burndown rate for output tokens on Bedrock quotas. With 2000+ word responses (current system), each debate turn burns ~5000 output tokens × 5 = 25000 from TPM quota. Reducing max_tokens and enforcing concise responses is essential for concurrency.

Bedrock Structured Outputs: enforce JSON schema on responses via outputConfig.textFormat. Grammar cached 24h. Use to force debate agents to return structured {argument, signals, confidence} instead of free-form text - eliminates parsing failures and reduces token waste.

iMAD paper (AAAI 2026) shows selective debate triggering reduces tokens by 92% while improving accuracy 13.5%. Key insight: MAD only helps on ~10-19% of cases. Confidence scores alone are unreliable for deciding when to debate. Need interpretable features + classifier.

Cross-region inference profiles on Bedrock route traffic across multiple AWS regions automatically - boosts throughput for burst workloads like parallel debates. Global CRIS optimizes cost+throughput. Compatible with prompt caching.

CRITICAL: Plumbing Not People blog (ryanvan, May 2026) cites 11 papers showing multi-agent debate DEGRADES performance. Key stats: single-agent baseline 82.5% win rate vs best deliberation protocol 13.8%. Multi-agent systems consume 15x more tokens. Coordination overhead degrades sequential reasoning by 39-70%. Distributed info: 30.1% accuracy vs 80.7% for single agent with complete info. Recommendation: single agent with skills, not multi-agent teams.

iMAD convergence detection: Uses 41 interpretable features from structured self-critique (hedge words, certainty words, contrastive markers, syntactic depth, confidence scores). Lightweight MLP classifier decides debate trigger. Reduces tokens 92% while IMPROVING accuracy 13.5%. Key insight: only 5-19% of cases actually benefit from debate.

FINAL STATS: 717 completed calls, 28 hours total compute time (4 workers = 7h wall). P50=83s, P90=156s, P99=1588s. Call breakdown: 316 Team A + 270 Team B + 83 relevance gates + 28 judges + 23 agreement validations. Children spawned: R1=8, R2=11, R3=3, R4=6, R5=4, R6=3 - most spawn in rounds 1-2 meaning early detection could prevent depth explosion.

FINAL: Scalable oversight via recursive debate (Alignment Forum) shows recursive decomposition works but needs depth budgets. Prover-Estimator-Debate protocol decomposes complex problems into simpler subproblems. Key insight: recursive debates need explicit depth limits and budget allocation per branch.

COMPLETE: Full findings written. Key recommendations: (1) Direct Bedrock API = 10-50x speedup per call, (2) Prompt caching for growing truths = 2-3x, (3) Adaptive stopping KS-statistic = halve rounds, (4) Selective triggering = skip 44% of debates, (5) Depth budget cap=3 = prevent explosion. Combined: 7hrs → 20-40min.

FINAL SUMMARY: Full findings written. Key AWS-documented optimizations: (1) Converse API replaces subprocess = 2-6hr savings, (2) Prompt caching with 1hr TTL for established truths = 30-50% latency cut, (3) Structured outputs enforce concise JSON = 50-70% fewer tokens, (4) max_tokens=800 + 5x burndown awareness = 2x concurrency, (5) Cross-region inference for burst throughput, (6) ConverseStream for early termination. Combined estimate: 7hrs → 20-40min.

FINAL: Wrote comprehensive findings. Key takeaways: (1) Replace kiro-cli with AsyncAnthropicBedrock for 10-50x speedup, (2) Reduce rounds from 7 to 3 - more rounds REDUCE quality per arxiv 2502.19130, (3) Pre-screen with self-critique to skip 44%+ redundant debates per iMAD, (4) Prompt caching for 45-80% cost reduction on growing truths context, (5) Depth cap at 3 not 7. Combined estimate: 7hrs→20-40min.

FINAL: Comprehensive findings written. Key numbers: 28h total compute, P50=83s/P99=1588s per call, 46% both_correct waste, children mostly spawn R1-R2. Top 3 improvements: (1) Direct Bedrock API = 3x faster per call, (2) Pre-screening eliminates 46% waste, (3) Reduce to 3-4 rounds saves 40% calls. Combined estimate: 7h → 30-60min.

KIRO-CLI CONCURRENCY DATA: Cold start >60s, warm response ~15.7s floor (ACP protocol overhead). At 50 concurrent agents kiro-cli fails (rate limit). Claude Code direct to Bedrock: 100% success at 100 concurrent, flat 108s latency. Bedrock quota barely touched at 0.61%. Cost: $0.60/agent. Cache reads are 68% of tokens at 1/8th price. CRITICAL: kiro-cli has 15-20s latency FLOOR that cannot be reduced.

PROMPT CACHING: Kiro team improved cache hit from 37% to 90%, TTFT from 6s to 3s (50% reduction). Bedrock prompt caching: up to 85% latency reduction, 90% cost reduction on cached tokens. Cache reads BYPASS TPM quota. 5-min TTL, 1024 token minimum. Floating cachepoint strategy recommended. For debate system: cache the growing ESTABLISHED TRUTHS as static prefix.

ADAPTIVE DEBATE TERMINATION: RL-based debate moderation (jinzhanj paper) uses Debate-RM for quality-driven termination. Threshold-based: additional rounds only when quality below threshold. CQL variant achieved +198 BPS over fixed rounds. Sycophancy research (Yao 2025): cap debates at 2-3 rounds max. Correct→incorrect flips OUTNUMBER incorrect→correct in later rounds. Social pressure increases over rounds.
